item.title: Joe Rogan Compares Some U.S. Military Commanders' Religious Extremism to Suicide Bombers Amid Iran War Comments
item.description: Podcast host Joe Rogan, a 2024 Trump supporter, expressed strong criticism of certain U.S. military commanders during a recent episode of 'The Joe Rogan Experience' with guest Mark Norman. Rogan highlighted reports from the Military Religious Freedom Foundation (MRFF), which has fielded numerous complaints from service members at over 30 installations and dozens of units. These complaints allege that some high-level commanders are portraying the ongoing U.S. war in Iran as fulfillment of a divine biblical plan, specifically urging troops not to worry because President Trump has been 'anointed by Jesus Christ' to trigger Armageddon through actions in Iran, hastening Jesus' return to Earth. One detailed complaint, first reported by journalist Jonathan Larsen, described a commander citing the Book of Revelation and instructing NCOs to convey this message to their subordinates. Rogan, discussing the contrast between moderate religion and extremism, directly quoted the claim and declared th

item.title: Police bodycam footage reveals details of Justin Timberlake’s 2024 DUI-related traffic stop
item.description:
Newly released police body camera footage provides a detailed look at a June 2024 traffic stop involving singer Justin Timberlake in Sag Harbor, New York. Officers pulled Timberlake over after observing him allegedly run a stop sign and drift out of his lane. According to police, he exited his vehicle smelling of alcohol, which prompted further investigation.
The video shows Timberlake undergoing a series of field sobriety tests, including walking heel-to-toe and balancing on one leg. During the tests, he appeared nervous and had difficulty following instructions, at one point apologizing and stating that his heart was racing. He also told officers he was in town for his world tour and identified himself during the stop.
After being taken into custody, Timberlake questioned why he was being arrested and later reacted with surprise upon learning he would be held overnight. The footage, which spans several hours, offers a comprehensive view of the interaction between the singer and law

item.title: Trump Administration Issues 30-Day Sanctions Waiver for Iranian Oil Sales at Sea to Ease Global Price Pressures Amid Ongoing War
item.description: The Trump administration announced a 30-day waiver on sanctions, allowing the purchase and sale of Iranian oil already loaded on vessels at sea. This move, effective until April 19, 2026, aims to release approximately 140 million barrels into global markets to relieve supply pressures and help lower oil prices, which have surged above $100 per barrel—the highest since 2022—due to the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran, known as Operation Epic Fury. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated on X that the waiver would bring much-needed supply to markets, particularly benefiting buyers in Asia like China, while emphasizing that Iran would face difficulties accessing revenues and that 'maximum pressure' on Tehran would continue. The license permits imports into the U.S. only if necessary for completing sales or deliveries, though meaningful U.S. imports of Iranian oil remain unlikely given historical restrictions since 1979. Exclusions apply to regions like Cuba, North Korea, and Cr

item.title: Study Links Fusobacterium nucleatum from Gum Disease to Promotion of Breast Cancer in Preclinical Models
item.description: Researchers from Johns Hopkins Medicine have uncovered a potential connection between a common oral bacterium associated with periodontal disease, Fusobacterium nucleatum, and breast cancer development and progression. The study, published in Cell Communication and Signaling, demonstrates that this microbe can enter the bloodstream, colonize breast tissue, and contribute to cancer by inducing inflammation, causing DNA damage, and promoting erroneous DNA repair mechanisms that lead to mutations. In mouse models mimicking human breast cancer, introduction of the bacterium accelerated tumor growth, increased cell proliferation, and enhanced metastasis, particularly to the lungs. The research also highlighted heightened vulnerability in cells with BRCA1 gene mutations, which express more Gal-GalNAc surface sugars that facilitate bacterial attachment and internalization, allowing persistent effects across cell generations. These findings build on prior observational studies linking poor ora

item.title: Justin Timberlake Pleads Guilty to Impaired Driving After Hamptons Arrest; Police Video Released
item.description: Police in Sag Harbor, New York, have released body camera footage showing pop star Justin Timberlake struggling with field sobriety tests during his 2024 arrest for suspected drunk driving. The video shows Timberlake having difficulty walking a straight line and standing on one leg while explaining he had consumed a single martini and was following friends home. Timberlake identified himself to officers as being on a world tour and apologized multiple times during the encounter, citing nervousness and a racing heart. After being taken to the police station, he was held overnight and requested to keep the cell light on. Initially, Timberlake’s lawyers attempted to block the release of the footage, citing privacy concerns and potential damage to his reputation, but later agreed to a redacted release in compliance with New York’s Freedom of Information Law. Timberlake ultimately pleaded guilty to a reduced charge of impaired driving in September 2024. As part of his plea deal, he complete

item.title: Meta drops end‑to‑end encryption for Instagram DMs, raising privacy concerns
item.description: Meta has announced that it will discontinue end‑to‑end encryption (E2EE) for Instagram direct messages, removing the feature entirely on May 8, 2026. The company says that very few users opted into the optional E2EE feature, so it is being phased out and users who want encrypted messaging are directed to WhatsApp instead. Security experts and privacy advocates argue that the move sets a troubling precedent at a time when governments worldwide are pushing to weaken encryption in the name of combating terrorism, child sexual abuse, and human trafficking. Meta spent years promising to roll out default E2EE across its chat platforms, including Instagram, and critics say the company deliberately kept the Instagram option hidden and hard to find, then used low adoption as an excuse to roll it back. Johns Hopkins cryptographer Matt Green and other researchers warn that Meta’s reversal could embolden other tech companies and even other Meta divisions to do less on encryption. Internal document

item.title: Iran-Israel Strikes Disrupt Key Oil and Gas Infrastructure in Persian Gulf
item.description: The ongoing war between Israel and Iran has escalated dramatically, with recent strikes targeting critical oil and gas facilities in the Persian Gulf region. Israel hit Iran's South Pars gas field, the world's largest, jointly operated with Qatar, while Iran retaliated against Qatar's major LNG export terminal. These attacks have damaged key infrastructure, with QatarEnergy reporting a 17% capacity loss for the next five years and declaring force majeure on contracts to Europe and Asia. The Strait of Hormuz, vital for 20% of global oil and LNG shipments, remains effectively closed, halting exports from major OPEC nations and disrupting supplies of petrochemicals and fertilizers. Oil prices have surged past $100 per barrel, peaking near $120, marking the first time since 2022's Ukraine crisis. Analysts like Rory Johnston and Ellen Wald describe this as a nightmarish 'thought experiment' turned reality, potentially causing COVID-level demand destruction without a pandemic—leading to no c

item.title: U.S. Sanctions Waiver Allows Iranian Oil Sales at Sea but Blocks Payments
item.description: The U.S. Treasury announced a limited sanctions waiver on Friday, March 20, 2026, permitting the sale, transportation, and unloading of Iranian oil already loaded onto ships before that date. This measure aims to ease rising global oil prices amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict disrupting energy markets. However, geopolitical analyst Fernando Ferreira from Rapidan Energy Group in Washington states that this exemption is unlikely to significantly increase available oil barrels because Iran cannot receive payments due to ongoing financial transaction bans. The waiver does not lift prohibitions on dealings with Iranian banks or entities, maintaining pressure on Tehran's economy. Iran primarily exports its oil to China, which has developed workarounds like alternative payment channels to bypass U.S. sanctions. Ferreira notes there is little incentive for Iran to utilize this waiver when its existing arrangements with China function effectively. Other Asian buyers, such as India, Jap

item.title: Rep. Jim Himes Lobbies Democrats to Renew FBI's Warrantless Section 702 Surveillance Authority Amid Oversight Concerns
item.description: Congressman Jim Himes, the ranking Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee, is privately urging fellow Democrats to support the renewal of Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act, a program that allows warrantless surveillance of foreign targets but incidentally collects vast amounts of Americans' communications. In a letter obtained by WIRED, Himes argues that reforms passed in 2024, including 56 measures to limit FBI misuse, are functioning effectively, with a reported compliance rate over 99 percent in recent years. He claims no evidence of abuse under the current Trump administration and FBI Director Kash Patel, emphasizing the program's importance for national security and the risks of expiration. However, critics highlight significant changes that undermine these safeguards: the dissolution of the FBI's Office of Internal Auditing, which previously exposed improper searches; the removal of civil service protections for key personnel; and reliance on self-rep

item.title: Trump Signals Potential Drawdown in Iran Conflict Amid Mixed Messages
item.description: President Trump may be signaling a potential winding down of the U.S. military involvement in Iran, though his statements remain inconsistent. On the White House south lawn, Trump claimed that the U.S. had achieved significant military successes, including degrading Iran's navy, air force, and missile capabilities. He emphasized that the U.S. had achieved victory but initially dismissed the idea of a ceasefire. Later, on Air Force One, he posted on Truth Social that the U.S. was 'considering winding down' military operations as objectives were close to being met. His listed accomplishments include preventing Iran from nearing nuclear capability, destroying military infrastructure, and protecting U.S. allies in the Middle East. However, analysts caution that Iran still retains nuclear material and the regime remains in power, raising the risk of retaliatory actions. Trump also indicated that maintaining security in the strategically crucial Hormuz Strait should be the responsibility of

item.title: Jury Finds Musk Liable for Misleading Twitter Investors During 2022 Buyout Dispute
item.description: A California civil jury has determined that Elon Musk intentionally misled Twitter investors during his attempt to withdraw from his $44 billion acquisition of the company in 2022. The case stems from Musk’s public remarks on Twitter (now X), where he questioned the platform’s number of fake or spam accounts. On May 13, 2022, Musk tweeted that the deal was 'temporarily on hold' pending verification that fake accounts made up less than 5% of users. Following his statements, Twitter’s stock value dropped 8%, prompting investor Giuseppe Pampena to file a class-action lawsuit on behalf of shareholders who sold their stock between Musk’s tweet and the eventual completion of the deal on October 4, 2022. Pampena alleged that Musk’s comments were strategically designed to influence Twitter’s share price and create market uncertainty. Although Musk’s defense argued his concerns were genuine, the jury sided with the plaintiffs. Damages could total as much as $2.6 billion, though this figure rema

item.title: Highlights and Key Moments from Friday’s NCAA March Madness Games
item.description: Friday’s NCAA March Madness action delivered thrilling games, dramatic buzzer-beaters, and some close calls. Kentucky’s Otega Oweh hit a long 3-pointer at the buzzer to force overtime against Santa Clara, eventually leading the Wildcats to an 89-84 victory after multiple lead changes. In the West Region, No. 9 Utah State defeated No. 8 Villanova 86-76, with coach Kevin Willard making headlines with a joking remark about firing his staff due to defensive mistakes. The Midwest saw No. 2 Iowa State dominate Tennessee State 108-74, but their second-team All-American Joshua Jefferson suffered a left ankle sprain. Meanwhile, No. 3 Virginia narrowly escaped Wright State 82-73 after an 8-0 run late in the game. In the women’s tournament, Baylor edged Nebraska 67-62 in a competitive matchup that featured multiple lead changes and late-game turnovers. Other men’s games on Friday included wins by Texas Tech, Arizona, Alabama, and Virginia, with upcoming matchups featuring Clemson vs. Iowa, St. Jo

item.title: ICE Arrests Defendant During Federal Court Hearing, Raising Legal Concerns
item.description: In an unusual and controversial incident in Los Angeles, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents removed a criminal defendant, Orlando Olivar, from a federal courtroom immediately after a pre-trial hearing. Olivar, accused of being a leader in the MS-13 gang and facing charges including racketeering conspiracy and drug distribution, was taken into custody without the agents identifying themselves or showing a warrant. The defense attorney expressed concerns about the safety of courthouses and the chilling effect on witnesses and defendants participating in the judicial system. ICE confirmed that Olivar, described as a 'criminal illegal alien,' had previously been removed from the U.S. and had reentered illegally multiple times. He is now held at the Adelanto ICE Processing Center pending deportation to El Salvador. Legal experts and former prosecutors noted that arrests inside courtrooms are highly unusual and could undermine public trust in the justice system. Recent ICE prac

item.title: Cuba Warns of Strong Resistance Amid Escalating U.S. Rhetoric and Sanctions
item.description:
The article describes rising tensions between the United States and Cuba following statements attributed to former President Donald Trump suggesting the possibility of taking control of the island. Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel strongly condemned these remarks, accusing the U.S. of maintaining a decades-long economic blockade designed to destabilize the country and justify intervention. He argued that Washington is intensifying sanctions, restricting fuel supplies, and worsening living conditions for ordinary Cubans while framing the resulting hardship as evidence that the government should be replaced.
Díaz-Canel warned that any attempt at foreign intervention would be met with what he called “impregnable resistance,” emphasizing Cuba’s commitment to sovereignty. The article also highlights comments from U.S. officials, including Marco Rubio, interpreted by Cuban authorities as support for regime change.
The report notes that economic conditions in Cuba have deteriorated, with w

item.title: Dr. Gordon Edwards Criticizes Overly Optimistic Claims About Nuclear Energy
item.description:
The article 'Debunking Nuclear “Hopium” – Dr. Gordon Edwards,' published on the website Nuclear News, examines the skepticism of Dr. Gordon Edwards toward the overly optimistic narratives surrounding the future of nuclear energy. Dr. Edwards, a long-time critic of the atomic industry and president of the Canadian Coalition for Nuclear Responsibility, questions recent claims portraying nuclear power—especially new concepts like small modular reactors (SMRs)—as a clean, safe, and cost-effective solution to global energy and climate challenges. He argues that such perspectives often downplay or ignore persistent problems related to radioactive waste management, the high cost of construction and decommissioning, safety risks, and the potential for proliferation of nuclear materials.
The piece situates this viewpoint within a broader context of public debate in Canada and internationally, where nuclear lobbyists advocate expansion of nuclear infrastructure as part of net-zero carbon strat

item.title: Technical Details of China's Lanyue Lunar Module and Its Landing Process
item.description:
The Chinese lunar module Lanyue (揽月), weighing 26 tons, represents the cornerstone of China’s first crewed lunar landing mission, expected within four years. Designed to work with the Mengzhou-Y spacecraft, the Lanyue will transport two astronauts to the lunar surface while a third crew member remains in orbit, following a mission architecture similar to Apollo. The system’s mass is limited by the Long March 10 (CZ-10) rocket, which can place around 27 tons on a trans-lunar trajectory (TLI). The vehicle consists of a Propulsion Module (PM) and a Lunar Module (LM), with the PM bringing the spacecraft into low lunar orbit and performing the braking burn before being discarded in a crasher stage configuration. The PM uses a single YF-58 engine, while the LM relies on four YF-36 variable-thrust engines similar to those of previous Chang’e missions.
Lanyue is capable of up to four days of surface operations, carrying 200 kg of consumables for two astronauts. It can wait in lunar orbit for

item.title: Microsoft Reduces Copilot AI Integrations in Windows 11 to Improve User Experience
item.description: Microsoft announced a series of adjustments to Windows 11 aimed at improving performance and usability by scaling back its integration of Copilot, the company’s AI assistant. Executive Vice President of Windows and Devices, Pavan Davuluri, explained that Microsoft intends to integrate AI 'where it’s most meaningful,' focusing on making the technology genuinely useful rather than omnipresent. As part of this change, Copilot features will be reduced or removed from apps such as Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and the Snipping Tool. The move follows growing criticism from users who felt the operating system had become cluttered with unnecessary AI components. It also comes amid broader concerns about trust, security, and the overuse of artificial intelligence. A Pew Research study in 2025 found that half of U.S. adults are more concerned than excited about AI, a sharp increase from 2021. Microsoft has previously reconsidered its AI push, including delaying and reworking its Windows Recall featu

item.title: 19-year-old Mexican dies by apparent suicide in Florida ICE detention center
item.description: A 19-year-old Mexican national, Royer Perez-Jimenez, has died in the custody of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) at the Glades County Detention Center in southern Florida. ICE reported that officers found Perez-Jimenez unresponsive early Monday morning, and lifesaving measures failed to revive him. Although the agency has described his death as a 'presumed suicide', an official investigation is still underway. Perez-Jimenez had been detained for about a month after being arrested in Volusia County, Florida, on charges of impersonating and resisting an officer. He became the youngest person to die in ICE custody since the beginning of Donald Trump’s second presidential term in January 2025, and the 46th overall. His death is also the 13th in ICE facilities so far this year and the second in just one week. Mexican authorities have condemned the death as unacceptable and have requested a thorough investigation, while consular officials in Miami have demanded detailed documen

item.title: 18 Notable Examples of Biophilic Architecture Reshaping Our Cities
item.description: Biophilic architecture, which blends natural elements into urban environments, is rapidly becoming a mainstream necessity rather than a mere environmental gesture. This design philosophy highlights the importance of incorporating nature into buildings for physiological benefits, such as reduced stress, improved cognitive function, and accelerated healing. The article showcases 18 remarkable examples of biophilic architecture from around the world, emphasizing how nature can enhance both the aesthetic and functional aspects of urban spaces. Notable projects include Bosco Verticale in Milan, which integrates over 20,000 plants into two residential towers, and the Jewel Changi Airport in Singapore, a shopping hub transformed into an indoor rainforest with the world’s tallest indoor waterfall. Other highlighted designs, like Amazon's Spheres in Seattle and the Parkroyal on Pickering in Singapore, demonstrate how businesses and hotels are embracing biophilic design to improve well-being and

item.title: Keeping your eyes open can improve hearing in noisy environments
item.description: A recent study from Shanghai Jiao Tong University challenges the common belief that closing your eyes helps you hear better. Researchers tested participants' ability to detect faint sounds under different visual conditions while background noise played. Surprisingly, closing the eyes made it harder for participants to hear subtle sounds. Using EEG measurements, the study found that eye closure puts the brain into a state called neural criticality, which increases filtering of incoming sounds, sometimes suppressing the very sounds participants were trying to detect. Conversely, seeing relevant visuals, such as a video matching the sound, improved hearing sensitivity. This suggests that visual engagement helps the brain anchor auditory perception to the external environment. While closing the eyes may still be useful in quiet settings, in noisy environments keeping the eyes open is more effective. Future research will explore whether the hearing improvement comes from simply having visua

item.title: NASA Sets Early April Window for Artemis II Moon Mission as Crew Quarantines
item.description:
NASA’s Artemis II mission, the first planned crewed lunar voyage in over 50 years, is returning to the launchpad after a series of technical delays. Following a setback in February caused by a liquid hydrogen leak during a rehearsal, NASA engineers have repositioned the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and the Orion spacecraft at Launch Pad 39B within Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The agency is now aiming for a launch window between April 1 and April 6, 2026. According to NASA, a live video feed shows the rocket securely in place, ready for final checks before liftoff.
The Artemis II mission is a critical step in NASA's broader Artemis program, which aims to build a lunar space station called the Lunar Gateway. The four-member crew consists of U.S. astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, as well as Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen. They recently entered pre-launch quarantine in Houston, Texas, to ensure their health before the mission.
During the 10-day flight,

item.title: San Diego County Water Authority Signs $100 Million Deal to Sell Water to Riverside County
item.description:
The San Diego County Water Authority (SDCWA) has entered into a long-term agreement to sell water to the Western Municipal Water District in Riverside County. The deal, expected to generate about $100 million over the first five years, will transfer at least 10,000 acre-feet of water annually for the next 21 years—enough to supply approximately 30,000 households. The arrangement is intended both to help Western Municipal meet growing water demands in areas such as Corona, Riverside, and Temecula, and to reduce the financial strain of high water costs for the 3.3 million residents served by SDCWA.
Over the past decades, SDCWA has invested heavily in water supply diversification, including desalination and agricultural-to-urban water transfers. These initiatives have increased reliability but also led to some of California’s highest-priced water. Meanwhile, local conservation has reduced overall demand, leaving the agency with excess supply that can now be sold.
The water will be con

item.title: Super Micro Co-Founder Accused of Illegally Exporting AI Servers With Nvidia Chips to China
item.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice has charged Yih-Shyan 'Wally' Liaw, co-founder of Super Micro Computer, along with Ruei-Tsang 'Steven' Chang and Ting-Wei 'Willy' Sun, for illegally exporting servers containing Nvidia’s advanced AI chips to China. The indictment alleges that the men smuggled approximately $2.5 billion worth of high-performance servers in violation of U.S. export control laws. According to prosecutors, the defendants used a pass-through company based in Southeast Asia to disguise the final destination of the products. They allegedly falsified documents, repackaged servers into unmarked boxes, and used 'dummy' nonfunctional servers to deceive auditors checking for compliance. Surveillance footage reportedly showed individuals altering product labels and serial numbers to conceal the shipments' true nature.
Liaw was arrested in California and released on bail, Sun remains detained pending a hearing, while Chang, based in Taiwan, is still at large. The DOJ maintains that t

item.title: Jon Stewart and Elon Musk Engage in Public Dispute Over Social Media and Democracy
item.description: This week, comedian Jon Stewart and billionaire Elon Musk engaged in a very public clash over social media and its impact on American democracy. The exchange began when Stewart aired a segment on “The Weekly Show,” criticizing Musk and his social media platform X for influencing political discourse. Stewart specifically challenged Musk's emphasis on undocumented voting, labeling it exaggerated, and highlighted the platform’s role in shaping public opinion and spreading misinformation. Stewart argued that Musk's control over algorithms makes him a major player in distorting democratic processes. Musk responded quickly on social media, calling Stewart a skilled propagandist, which Stewart countered by labeling Musk the same. The back-and-forth escalated online with both taking jabs at each other, though Stewart later offered an olive branch, inviting Musk to appear on his show to discuss their differences in a more constructive setting. This follows prior attempts at an interview, which

item.title: Australia's Quiet Adoption of Palantir Software and Its Implications for Democracy
item.description: In March 2026, Palantir Technologies embedded itself within Australia’s intelligence and financial systems under the guise of a software upgrade. This system update, which bypassed parliamentary debate, integrates vast amounts of data from banks, law enforcement, and government agencies into a central 'God view' through AUSTRAC and the Fintel Alliance. The Australian government has invested in Palantir through the Future Fund, positioning it to become a global operating system for governments. This shift signifies a move away from social democracy towards AI-driven decision-making, where efficiency and speed take precedence over human judgment and empathy. Critics argue that this transformation, although framed as a necessary evolution, subtly edges Australia closer to a technocratic system, outsourcing decision-making to algorithms. The article raises concerns about the lack of public debate and the potential dangers of such a system, particularly given the views of Palantir’s founder

item.title: Trump-Appointed Counterterrorism Chief Joe Kent Resigns Over U.S. Military Actions in Iran
item.description: Joe Kent, the Trump-appointed Director of the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC), resigned on March 17, 2026, citing moral opposition to U.S. military operations in Iran. A retired Green Beret with extensive combat experience and a former CIA paramilitary officer, Kent stated that Iran posed no immediate threat to the United States. He attributed the war to external pressure from Israel and influential American lobbyists. Kent emphasized that his decision aligns with the 'America First' principles that Trump promoted during his campaigns, noting Trump’s prior restraint in Middle East conflicts to avoid costly and prolonged engagements. His resignation highlights internal divisions within the Republican Party, particularly among lawmakers and MAGA supporters who oppose the conflict. Figures such as Rep. Thomas Massie, Sen. Rand Paul, and Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene have criticized the campaign, comparing it to past U.S. missteps in Iraq and Afghanistan. Kent’s departure as a Senate

item.title: Impact of the Iran War on Global Energy and U.S. Policy
item.description: The article from Sky News explores the global fallout from escalating hostilities between Iran and Israel, particularly following two major attacks targeting vital energy plants in the Middle East. One strike was carried out by Israeli forces, while the other was retaliatory action by Iran. These assaults have severely disrupted international energy networks, driving up oil prices and stoking fears of economic instability across multiple regions dependent on Middle Eastern fuel supplies. Analysts warn that this confrontation marks one of the most significant disruptions to global energy flows in recent years. The piece also covers a U.S. political dimension — with Pentagon spokesperson Pete Hegseth urging Congress for hundreds of billions of dollars in new funding to sustain what could become another prolonged American military engagement, a scenario reminiscent of the so-called 'forever wars.' Furthermore, the article touches on a growing concern within Washington about the administra

item.title: Carhartt’s Midweight Pocket Tee Stands Out for Quality, Comfort, and Durability
item.description: HuffPost highlights a seasonal sale on Carhartt’s midweight short-sleeved pocket T-shirt, a long-standing favorite among shoppers for its quality, comfort, and longevity. Currently priced at around $21 on Amazon, this classic crewneck tee is available in 33 colors and offers functional features like sweat-wicking technology, ventilated side panels, and UPF 25+ sun protection. Reviewers describe it as durable enough to last nearly a decade, with many praising its comfort and versatility across seasons and activities. Designed with a relaxed fit and available in sizes from S to 4XL, including tall options, the shirt suits a wide range of body types and use cases—from outdoor work to everyday wear. The article includes customer testimonials emphasizing the shirt’s ability to maintain color, fit, and shape after years of wear and washing. Its breathable and durable fabric has made it a go-to option for both casual and professional use. Alongside the Carhartt deal, the article also recommen

item.title: Charles Bennett and Gilles Brassard Receive Turing Award for Founding Quantum Information Theory
item.description: Charles Bennett and Gilles Brassard, pioneers in quantum information theory, have been awarded the ACM A.M. Turing Award, the highest honor in computer science. Their collaboration began in 1979 during a chance meeting off the coast of Puerto Rico, where they discussed applying quantum mechanics to cryptography, an idea originally inspired by a classmate of Bennett's who envisioned uncounterfeitable digital money in the 1960s. Together, Bennett and Brassard developed the BB84 protocol, laying the foundation for quantum cryptography. Their work demonstrated that quantum phenomena, once considered a nuisance in electronics, could be harnessed to solve problems in computing. While quantum computing as a field was independently conceived, the duo contributed significantly to its development, including designing mechanisms for quantum teleportation and influencing thoughts on a future quantum internet. Today, their theories are deeply embedded in the ongoing development of quantum computers

item.title: A Critique of Pope Leo's Stance on Artificial Intelligence and Its Global Implications
item.description: The article discusses the unexpected admiration of Pope Leo XIV by the author, despite their past rejection of Catholicism. It focuses on Pope Leo's cautious approach to artificial intelligence (AI), encouraging the use of human intellect over AI when delivering sermons. The Pope has also called for ethical responsibility in technological advancements, particularly in AI. While the author appreciates the Pope's stance, the article delves into the concerns of tech billionaire Peter Thiel, who sees the Pope's influence as a potential threat to the technological empire he represents. Thiel warns of a potential fusion between government and religious leadership, predicting the rise of an 'Antichrist' figure, though he does not directly associate Pope Leo with the Antichrist. The article highlights the philosophical clash between traditional religious perspectives on technology and the interests of influential tech leaders like Thiel.

item.title: Senate Republicans Claim Democratic Resistance Is Driven by Far-Left Pressure During DHS Shutdown
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune, a Republican from South Dakota, suggested that the ongoing Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown is being prolonged because Senate Democrats are pressured by their far-left base. The DHS has been partially closed for 35 days, a record-breaking duration, leading to long lines at airports and rising national security concerns. Thune stated that Democrats' demands include defunding ICE and law enforcement, positions he considers unreasonable. He criticized Democrats for previously avoiding negotiations and suggested that publicizing White House offers to Democrats helped prompt recent discussions. Although some Democratic negotiators met with Senate Republicans and border officials this week, Thune remained cautious about whether a resolution is imminent. Meanwhile, Republican senators like Susan Collins and Katie Britt emphasized that Democrats’ demands keep expanding, complicating efforts to reach an agreement. The article underscores politica

item.title: Tinder Launches New Features to Improve Online Dating Experience
item.description: Tinder, the world’s most popular dating app, is rolling out a series of new features aimed at revitalizing the online dating experience, particularly for Gen Z and women, amid declining engagement. CEO Spencer Rascoff is focusing on low-pressure, social connections rather than simply increasing matches. Among the updates are 'Double Date,' which allows users to pair with friends for joint swiping, 'Astrology Mode' for zodiac-based compatibility, and 'Chemistry,' an AI tool analyzing users’ camera rolls to understand interests and personality traits without storing the images. Safety features have also been enhanced, including AI moderation for potentially harmful or profane messages, and mandatory Face Check verification to combat fake accounts, which remain a major challenge. Tinder’s AI-driven algorithm rebuilds user matching and monitors nuanced language patterns to distinguish playful from abusive interactions. However, challenges persist: marginalized users continue to face harass

item.title: Actor and martial artist Chuck Norris dies at 86 after decades of shaping action cinema
item.description:
Chuck Norris, the American martial artist, actor, and cultural icon best known for his role as Cordell Walker in the long-running TV series 'Walker, Texas Ranger,' has passed away at the age of 86. According to a statement released by his family on social media, Norris died peacefully at home in Hawaii after a sudden medical emergency. His loved ones described him as a man of faith, discipline, and kindness who left a deep impact on millions around the world.
Born Carlos Ray Norris in Ryan, Oklahoma, Norris had Irish American and Cherokee ancestry. After his parents’ divorce, he moved with his mother and brothers to Kansas and later to California. He discovered martial arts during his service with the U.S. Air Force in Korea in the late 1950s, eventually developing his own fighting style known as the Chuck Norris System. He became a world karate champion and trained celebrities like Steve McQueen, Priscilla Presley, and Bob Barker.
Norris transitioned into acting in the early 1970s,

item.title: Opera GX Gaming Browser Launches Native Support for Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, and openSUSE
item.description: Opera GX, a gaming-focused web browser developed by Opera, has officially launched for Linux distributions including Debian, Ubuntu, Fedora, and openSUSE-based systems. Announced on March 20, 2026, via a Slashdot article linking to nerds.xyz, the browser brings its unique GX Control features to Linux users, allowing them to limit RAM and network usage to optimize system performance during gaming or heavy multitasking. Additional tools like the Hot Tabs Killer automatically close resource-intensive tabs, while built-in sidebar integrations provide seamless access to Discord and Twitch without switching applications. Opera emphasizes this as a long-term commitment, with weekly updates planned and active community engagement through Discord, forums, and bug reporting. Maciej Kocemba, Product Director at Opera GX, highlighted the shift in PC gaming away from dominant platforms, noting Linux users' preference for fine-tuned control over their tools. This release supports installation via .d

item.title: China Approves First Brain Implant for Sale, Paving the Way for Global Industry Leadership
item.description: In a groundbreaking move, China has become the first country to approve a commercially available brain implant, the NEO chip, designed to help individuals with paralysis regain mobility. Developed by Neuracle Medical Technology, the chip translates brain signals into actions for a prosthetic hand, enabling users to perform basic tasks like picking up objects and manipulating utensils. After 18 months of successful testing, the Chinese National Medical Products Administration has given the green light to the implant for people aged 19 to 60 suffering from paralysis due to neck or spinal injuries. The approval marks a major milestone in the brain-computer interface (BCI) industry, which has seen several experimental devices, such as Neuralink, struggle to obtain regulatory approval due to side effects. Additionally, China has outlined a comprehensive plan to lead the global BCI market, with strategies to mass-produce non-invasive devices and test them in high-risk sectors. This move unde

item.title: Record March Heat Sweeps U.S. Southwest Amid Expanding Heat Dome
item.description: Preliminary weather readings show that the United States has experienced its hottest March day on record, with extreme heat striking Arizona, southern California, and other parts of the Southwest. Temperatures reached about 43°C (110°F), marking an unprecedented early-season heatwave driven by a large heat dome—an area of persistent high pressure that traps hot air beneath it. Scientists from World Weather Attribution reported that such extreme March heat would have been virtually impossible without human-caused climate change. Andrew Weaver, a climate scientist at the University of Victoria, emphasized that this event illustrates how climate change is creating new extremes beyond what was previously considered possible. The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration noted that in the past five years, the area of the U.S. affected by extreme weather has doubled compared to two decades ago. More than 38 million people across the Desert Southwest are currently under heat alerts. Pho

item.title: UK Government Freezes Student Loan Repayment Threshold, Increasing Burden on Plan 2 Borrowers
item.description: Recent changes to England's student loan system, announced in Chancellor Rachel Reeves' November 2025 budget, have sparked significant backlash among graduates. The core issue involves Plan 2 loans, introduced in 2012 when tuition fees rose to £9,000 annually, affecting around five million borrowers who represent about 80% of the £240 billion student loan book. These loans require repayments of 9% of income above a threshold (set to reach £29,385 in April 2026), with interest accruing at RPI inflation plus up to 3% based on earnings—terms far costlier than earlier plans. The budget imposes a three-year freeze on the repayment threshold starting in 2027, halting inflation-linked increases and effectively raising lifetime repayments. The Institute for Fiscal Studies estimates an average £3,000 increase in total repayments, with lower earners hit hardest (up to £5,000 more) compared to higher earners (£700 extra). Many borrowers see their debt grow faster than repayments due to high inter

item.title: Artist Creates Miniature Felted Nudibranchs Inspired by Ocean Life
item.description: Russian artist Arina Borevich is captivating audiences with her exquisite hand-felted sculptures of nudibranchs, a type of vibrantly colored sea slug. Through her project, Wool Creature Lab, Borevich meticulously recreates these fascinating creatures from sheep’s wool, using a needle and immense patience. Each miniature sculpture takes between six and twelve hours to complete, building layer upon layer to capture the nudibranch's complex shapes and colors. Borevich's inspiration stems from her time working at a marine research station on Russia’s White Sea, where she was surrounded by scientists studying these unique mollusks. Initially created as small gifts, her hobby blossomed into a tribute to the ocean’s most flamboyant residents. To date, she has recreated over forty different species, each one a soft, tactile work of art. Borevich's long-term goal is truly ambitious: to felt every known species of nudibranch on Earth. These tiny, whimsical sculptures offer a unique and charming

item.title: Jelly Roll reflects on his marriage with Bunnie Xo, denying 'gold digger' claims and discussing past challenges
item.description:
Country star Jelly Roll has dismissed longtime jokes that his wife, Bunnie Xo, is a 'gold digger,' saying the claims couldn’t be further from the truth because he was homeless when they met. In a recent interview with Taste of Country Nights, the singer, known for hits like 'Save Me,' said he finds the accusation amusing even though his wife dislikes it. At the time of their meeting, Jelly Roll was living out of a 1996 conversion van and struggling to make ends meet, which he says makes the idea of her seeking his money absurd.
The couple, who married in 2016, have faced various challenges throughout their relationship. Jelly Roll’s life and career have changed dramatically since then, but their bond has been tested by both health scares and personal issues. Earlier this year, Bunnie Xo shared on Instagram that Jelly Roll was seriously injured in an ATV accident, but he has since fully recovered. In her recently released memoir, 'Stripped Down Unfiltered and Unapologetic,' Bunnie ope

item.title: How the U.S. arms industry profits from the war against Iran
item.description:
The article examines how war, while devastating societies and killing people, also creates lucrative opportunities for select sectors of the economy. It highlights that U.S. frackers benefit from higher oil prices when the barrel exceeds 100 dollars, making many Permian Basin wells profitable and driving new production. However, the piece argues that the most direct profiteers are the arms manufacturers, whose weapons are being used in the first 48 hours of what the author describes as an aggressive war against Iran.
The text points to major defense companies such as Raytheon (under RTX), Northrop Grumman (NOC), and Lockheed Martin (LMT), whose munition stocks must be replenished as they are consumed on the battlefield. U.S. President Donald Trump is said to have pressed industry leaders to accelerate production, turning each explosion in Iranian cities like Tehran, Isfahan, or Shiraz into a fresh source of revenue for firms headquartered in Arlington, Virginia—the same county that ho

item.title: Visceral Fat Raises Heart Failure Risk Independently of BMI
item.description: New research presented at the American Heart Association's EPI|Lifestyle Scientific Sessions 2026 highlights the link between belly fat, also known as visceral fat, and the risk of heart failure, even in individuals with a normal body mass index (BMI). The study, conducted using data from 1,998 African American adults in the Jackson Heart Study, found that waist circumference and waist-to-height ratio were more strongly associated with heart failure risk than overall body weight. Over a median follow-up period of 6.9 years, 112 participants developed heart failure, and those with higher inflammation levels were more likely to be affected. Inflammation appears to mediate roughly 25–33% of the connection between central adiposity and heart failure. Experts suggest that monitoring waist size and inflammation markers could help clinicians identify at-risk individuals earlier, enabling preventive strategies before symptoms emerge. While BMI has traditionally been used to assess obesity-rela

item.title: Weekly Political Cartoon Gallery Highlights Satire on Current U.S. Politics
item.description: This POLITICO feature presents a curated selection of the best political cartoons from across the United States, showcasing the work of talented artists from various publications and representing a wide range of political perspectives. Edited by Matt Wuerker, the gallery captures the foibles, memes, hypocrisies, and notable events dominating the American political landscape during the week of March 19, 2026. Every week, cartoonists apply their sharp wit and ink-stained skills to comment on the headlines, turning complex issues into visually striking and often humorous critiques that entertain and provoke readers of all stripes. The collection includes contributions from well-known creators such as Dave Whamond, Michael Ramirez, Mike Luckovich, Lalo Alcaraz, Clay Bennett, and many others, whose works appear in outlets ranging from Cagle.com and Tribune Content Agency to major newspapers like the Atlanta Journal-Constitution and Chattanooga Times Free Press. These cartoons serve as a bar

item.title: West Virginia man arrested after allegedly shooting father with a crossbow and fleeing in snow
item.description: Authorities in West Virginia arrested Chase Fleming after he allegedly shot his father in the face with a crossbow during a domestic dispute. The bolt reportedly entered just below the victim's left eye and exited the back of his head, but he remains in stable condition. Following the incident, Fleming fled the scene in his pickup truck, leading authorities on a multi-county manhunt through snowy and wooded areas of Jackson and Roane counties. The sheriff’s department utilized K-9 units and drones to cover approximately seven miles while searching for the suspect. Fleming was eventually found hiding under a rock on a remote ridge and was taken into custody without resistance. He was admitted to a hospital for observation before being medically cleared for incarceration. He has been charged with malicious assault, with additional charges pending. Bond has not yet been set. The sheriff described the survival of the victim as “really remarkable,” given the severity of the injury. The case

item.title: University of Alabama Student James Gracey Dies in Barcelona During Spring Break
item.description: James “Jimmy” Gracey, a 20-year-old junior at the University of Alabama, was found dead in Barcelona, Spain, after going missing during a spring break night out. Gracey had traveled overseas to visit classmates studying abroad, spending the weekend in Amsterdam before arriving in Barcelona. On the night of March 17, he went out with friends to the beachfront clubs in Port Olímpic. He was last seen at the club Shoko around 3 a.m. after separating from his friends. His family reported him missing when he failed to return to his rental. Spanish authorities, including marine and underwater units, conducted an extensive search near the club and the surrounding beaches. Gracey’s stolen phone was later recovered, and security footage from the club was reviewed, but details remain limited. His body was ultimately discovered a few meters underwater off Somorrostro beach, near the club where he had last been seen. Investigators say initial signs indicate an accidental death, though circumstances

item.title: Israel Commits to Halting Further Strikes on Iranian Energy Sites After Netanyahu-Trump Coordination
item.description: The US-Israel war against Iran has entered its third week, with significant developments in energy infrastructure attacks and global economic repercussions. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that Israel acted independently in striking a facility linked to Iran's South Pars natural gas field but will comply with US President Donald Trump's request to avoid repeating such attacks on key energy sites. This follows Iran's retaliatory strikes on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG facility and other regional targets, causing oil prices to surge above $119 per barrel before moderating slightly. The conflict has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz, effectively halting key shipping lanes and exacerbating global energy shortages. Goldman Sachs warned that elevated prices could persist through 2027. The war's ripple effects include scaled-back operations in gas-dependent industries in India, panic-buying of cooking alternatives, and warnings from the World Food Programme of potential record glob

item.title: China Supports Cuba in Expanding Solar Power Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: Cuba is rapidly expanding its solar energy capacity with significant support from China, as the Caribbean island faces its worst energy crisis in decades due to U.S. sanctions and limited oil imports. Chinese exports of solar technology to Cuba surged from around $5 million in 2023 to $117 million in 2025, demonstrating a strong commitment to aiding Cuba's renewable energy sector. Researchers from Ember estimate that solar energy could account for up to 10% of Cuba's electricity generation, making it one of the fastest-growing solar markets globally. This growth positions Cuba ahead of many countries, including the U.S., in terms of the proportion of electricity generated from solar power. China's involvement in Cuba is part of its broader strategy to expand its influence in renewable energy markets worldwide, especially as global energy prices rise amid geopolitical tensions such as the conflict involving Iran. Authorities in China have indicated plans to replicate similar projects in

item.title: High Intake of Ultra-Processed Foods Raises Risk of Heart Disease and Stroke
item.description: A recent study presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26) found that consuming large amounts of ultra-processed foods significantly increases the risk of major cardiovascular events. In the study, adults who ate approximately nine servings per day of foods such as chips, frozen meals, sugary drinks, and packaged snacks had a 67% higher risk of heart attack, stroke, or death from heart disease compared to those consuming only one serving per day. The research also found that each additional daily serving increased the risk by over 5%, independent of total calories, overall diet quality, or existing health conditions. The study analyzed data from 6,814 adults aged 45–84 in the Multi-Ethnic Study of Atherosclerosis (MESA), highlighting that Black Americans faced an even higher risk increase per serving. Researchers emphasized that factors like targeted marketing and limited access to minimally processed foods could contribute to disparities. While

item.title: Extreme Heat and a Looming El Niño Could Bring a Year of Unstable Weather Across the U.S.
item.description: The United States is facing the prospect of a tumultuous year for weather, as a record-breaking heat wave grips the West and forecasts suggest a strong El Niño may form later in 2026. Following 2025—the third-hottest year on record but relatively calm in terms of climate disasters—temperatures are now soaring far beyond seasonal norms. The National Weather Service has issued heat and fire warnings across much of the western half of the country, with some regions expected to see 85- to 90-degree highs in March, an extraordinary development for late winter. Climate scientist Daniel Swain of the University of California notes that this episode represents one of the strongest off-season ridges of high pressure ever observed, with extreme heat lasting more than a week in some areas. Already-low snowpack levels, in some places below 50 percent of normal, threaten the water supply for millions who depend on the Colorado River. This deficiency could worsen as the heat persists, heightening the

item.title: Guggenheim Cuts Disney Price Target to $115 While Maintaining Buy Rating
item.description:
Guggenheim has lowered its price target for The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) from $140 to $115 while maintaining its 'buy' rating, signaling continued confidence in the stock despite near-term challenges. The revised target suggests a 15.77% potential upside from Disney’s current price of about $99 per share. Other firms, including Citigroup and TD Cowen, have also trimmed their price objectives in recent months, keeping a generally positive but cautious outlook on the entertainment giant. According to MarketBeat data, Disney currently carries an average analyst rating of 'Moderate Buy' and a consensus price target of $134.13.
The company reported quarterly earnings of $1.63 per share, surpassing expectations by $0.06, on revenue of approximately $25.98 billion — up 5.2% year over year. With a market capitalization of roughly $176 billion, Disney maintains solid fundamentals with a net margin of 12.8% and return on equity of 8.9%. The firm’s debt-to-equity ratio remains low at 0.31

item.title: World Athletics selects India, China, and Kazakhstan to host major championships through 2030
item.description:
World Athletics has announced that India, Kazakhstan, and China will host major global athletics events between 2028 and 2030 as part of its strategy to expand the sport in emerging markets. During the 240th World Athletics Council Meeting held in Toruń, Poland, Odisha in India was chosen to host the 2028 World Athletics Indoor Championships, while Kazakhstan’s capital, Astana, will host the 2030 edition. Additionally, Hefei, China, will stage the 2028 World Athletics Race Walking Team Championships. These decisions aim to strengthen athletics’ global reach and inspire new generations of athletes in regions with growing sports infrastructure and engagement.
World Athletics President Sebastian Coe praised Poland’s continued contribution to athletics and emphasized that the new host locations align with the organization’s goal of promoting the sport worldwide. Odisha’s Chief Minister highlighted the state’s decade-long investment in sporting infrastructure and its vision to make sports

item.title: US Dismantles Four Major Botnets Behind Massive DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The United States Department of Justice (DOJ), in collaboration with the Defense Criminal Investigative Service and international partners from Canada and Germany, has dismantled four of the world’s largest botnets—Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad. These networks collectively infected more than 3 million devices around the globe, many of them inside home networks. According to cybersecurity firm Cloudflare, the Aisuru and Kimwolf botnets alone were responsible for some of the biggest distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks ever recorded, including a 31.4-terabit-per-second assault last November that nearly tripled previous volume records.
Aisuru primarily targeted internet-connected devices like DVRs, webcams, and network hardware, while Kimwolf focused on Android-based devices such as smart TVs and set-top boxes. Both were variants of Mirai, the infamous 2016 botnet that revolutionized IoT-based cyberattacks. Experts explained that these new strains used more advanced infe

item.title: Cloudflare CEO Predicts AI-Driven Bot Traffic Could Surpass Human Traffic by 2027
item.description: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince has forecasted that by 2027, AI-powered bots will generate more internet traffic than human users. According to Prince, AI agents can perform online tasks at massively higher volumes than humans, visiting hundreds or even thousands of websites while completing actions that a human might only perform on a few. Previously, bot traffic accounted for roughly 20% of total internet activity, with legitimate crawlers like Google being the primary contributors. However, the rise of generative AI has created a surge in automated web requests, driven by both legitimate and malicious agents. Prince emphasized that this shift requires new technological infrastructure, such as dynamic 'sandboxes' that can spin up code on demand to handle AI agents' tasks, including tasks like vacation planning. These sandboxes could be created at a scale of millions per second, reflecting a major shift in how information is consumed and processed online. Experts and commentators note t

item.title: Ongoing Iran‑US‑Israel War: Escalating Strikes, Regional Fallout and Diplomatic Tensions in 2026
item.description: The Wall Street Journal’s live coverage tracks the intensifying conflict between Iran, the United States, and Israel in early 2026, which erupted after a major joint U.S.–Israeli strike campaign known as Operation Epic Fury. The offensive, launched on February 28, involved hundreds of airstrikes targeting Iranian missile systems, air defenses, and key leadership figures, including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, whose reported death has further destabilized Tehran’s hierarchy. In retaliation, Iran has unleashed waves of ballistic missiles and drones at U.S. bases across the Gulf, Israel, and allied infrastructure, triggering widespread alarm in the region and prompting multiple intercept attempts by U.S., Israeli, and Gulf‑state air defenses. The conflict has already exacted heavy human and material costs, with fatalities reported in Iran, Israel, Lebanon, and neighboring countries, while civilian infrastructure such as schools, hospitals, and energy facilities has been damaged. The Trump

item.title: Kevin Spacey Reaches Confidential Settlement in Civil Sexual Assault Cases in London
item.description: Kevin Spacey, the Oscar-winning actor, has reached a confidential out-of-court settlement in civil cases filed by three British men who accused him of sexual assault. The claims, which were to be heard at London’s High Court in October 2026, have been halted after both parties agreed to terms earlier this month. A court order dated March 13 confirmed that the cases were stayed with consent and that proceedings would cease under the confidential terms of the agreement. The details of the settlement have not been disclosed. The men alleged Spacey assaulted them between 2000 and 2013, with two of them previously giving testimony in Spacey’s 2023 criminal trial at Southwark Crown Court, where he was acquitted of all charges. One of the claimants, Ruari Cannon, publicly accused the actor of assaulting him during a 2013 theater event. The barrister representing the plaintiffs mentioned that seven additional people had expressed willingness to testify. Spacey’s legal team has not commented on

item.title: European Cloud Providers Urge EU to Halt Broadcom’s Shutdown of VMware Partner Program
item.description:
A European trade group representing smaller cloud infrastructure providers has formally asked the European Commission to intervene in Broadcom’s decision to terminate VMware’s Cloud Service Provider (VCSP) program. The organization, CISPE, argues that the move threatens the survival of many smaller providers and reduces competition in the cloud services market. According to the complaint, Broadcom’s actions following its 2023 acquisition of VMware—including steep price increases, stricter payment terms, and bundled product requirements—have already placed significant strain on partners and customers alike.
The VCSP program, which allowed numerous cloud providers across Europe to resell VMware services, officially closed in January 2026, with all transactions required to be completed by March 31. After that deadline, only a limited number of authorized partners will be allowed to continue offering VMware subscriptions. CISPE claims this change could force hundreds of providers out of t

item.title: Autistic Man Blinded in One Eye by LAPD Less-Lethal Round at Anti-ICE Protest, Lawsuit Alleges
item.description: Jesus Javier Islas, a 23-year-old autistic man, filed a $100 million legal claim against the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) alleging officers blinded him in his right eye with a less-lethal munition during an anti-ICE protest on January 31, 2026, near the Metropolitan Detention Center in downtown Los Angeles. Video footage shows Islas standing still, leaning on a scooter on Alameda Street, when he was struck in the face by a projectile that exploded in green paint, causing him to scream and stumble in pain. No nearby confrontations between police and protesters are visible in the video, and Islas claims he posed no threat, having arrived just minutes earlier to see a friend after work. Doctors at L.A. County-USC Medical Center later confirmed permanent vision loss in his right eye, with concerns about potential complications for his left eye. LAPD acknowledged using foam baton rounds and FN 303 munitions that night, firing nearly three dozen rounds amid reports of protesters thro

item.title: University of Alabama student Jimmy Gracey found dead in Barcelona after search
item.description: Authorities in Barcelona, Spain, confirmed the recovery of 20-year-old University of Alabama student James 'Jimmy' Gracey's body after he went missing earlier in March. Gracey, a junior from Elmhurst, Illinois, was last seen around 3 a.m. at the Shoko nightclub and restaurant near Port Olimpic, a popular marina. After separating from friends at the end of the night, he did not return to the Airbnb they had rented. The search for Gracey involved multiple agencies and methods, including helicopters, boats, dive teams, and motorcycle units. Divers reported very limited visibility in the water, sometimes as low as 16 inches. Authorities recovered Gracey's phone during a separate arrest, and location data from the device is being reviewed as part of the ongoing investigation. The cause of death has not yet been released. Gracey's family expressed profound grief, describing him as a deeply loved son, grandson, brother, nephew, cousin, and friend. The discovery of his body has brought closure

item.title: Profiles of Iran’s Senior Leadership Amid Recent U.S. Bounty Announcements
item.description: On March 15, 2026, the Trump administration announced a $10 million reward for information on several senior Iranian figures. Two of these leaders have already been killed in Israeli strikes, highlighting the volatile context of Iran’s leadership dynamics. Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, was appointed Iran’s new supreme leader in early March. Though he never held formal office, he wielded significant influence within Iran’s security and intelligence networks. Seyyed Ali-Asghar Hejazi, a long-time cleric and intelligence operative, has served as a key intermediary in government and survived an Israeli attack earlier in March. Other notable figures include Seyyed Esmail Khatib, former intelligence minister killed on March 18, 2026, and Ali Larijani, a seasoned political insider assassinated on March 17, 2026. Brig. Gen. Eskandar Momeni, the current minister of interior, played a central role in domestic security operations, including recent lethal cr

item.title: Firms Linked to Trump Allies Earned $23 Million From DHS Advertising Contracts
item.description: Two media firms with longstanding ties to Republican political operatives earned at least $23 million in commissions from a controversial $220 million Department of Homeland Security (DHS) advertising campaign that contributed to the ousting of Secretary Kristi Noem. According to internal documents and sources, Safe America Media, formed just days before being awarded its contract, received at least $15.2 million. The firm was run by Mike McElwain and Patrick McCarthy, both connected to companies that previously worked for Donald Trump’s 2024 campaign. The second company, People Who Think, received around $7.7 million for a portion of the campaign’s international advertising work. Both contracts were issued under 'limited competition' due to an 'urgent and compelling need' for the campaign, according to a March 3 DHS memo, which stated the commissions were below typical industry rates. The campaign, one of the most expensive federal ad efforts in the past decade, drew criticism from la

item.title: 'The Price Is Right' Model Holly Hallstrom Details Firing and Legal Battle with Bob Barker
item.description: Holly Hallstrom, a former model on the long-running game show 'The Price Is Right,' has opened up about the circumstances surrounding her firing from the show in 1995. While the official reason given for her departure was her weight, Hallstrom claims in the new E! docuseries 'Dirty Rotten Scandals' that her dismissal was actually linked to her refusal to defend host Bob Barker in a sexual harassment case brought by fellow model Dian Parkinson. Hallstrom stated that she did not want to commit perjury in court on Barker's behalf and believes this was the real reason she was forced out. According to Hallstrom, lawyers eventually framed her exit as a retirement, but she maintains that Barker wanted her gone after nearly two decades on the show. The fallout from her firing led to a lengthy legal battle with Barker, including a defamation lawsuit he filed against her, which she fought for years. Hallstrom said the struggle drained her financially, causing her to sell her assets and live out

item.title: UK Government Allocates Funds to Remove Illegal Waste Sites in Northern England
item.description: The UK government has announced it will fund the removal of 48,000 tonnes of waste from illegal dumping sites in northern England, including locations in Wigan, Sheffield, and Lancashire. This decision follows criticism of a perceived north-south divide in addressing waste crime, as similar funding had previously been allocated to clear sites in Oxfordshire. The initiative is part of a broader plan by the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (DEFRA) and the Environment Agency (EA) described as the 'toughest ever crackdown' on waste criminals. Measures include faster intelligence processing, quicker response times to reports, and public naming of offenders. A new drone squad equipped with lasers, thermal imaging, and cameras will assist in monitoring and gathering evidence of illegal dumping. Officials aim to intervene earlier at dump sites to prevent large-scale accumulation, using restriction orders to halt activity immediately. Additional measures include creating clean

item.title: Severe Storms Cause Widespread Flight Disruptions and Tornado Warnings Across Eastern United States
item.description: Severe weather across the eastern United States caused widespread travel disruptions and prompted tornado watches in multiple states on Monday, March 16, 2026. Nearly 60 million people, from northern Florida to New York, were at risk of powerful storms capable of producing destructive winds and multiple tornadoes, according to the National Weather Service (NWS). Heavy snow continued further inland, affecting the Upper Midwest and Great Lakes regions. Federal authorities ordered government employees in Washington, D.C., to leave work early due to the risk of dangerous conditions, while the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) temporarily grounded flights at major airports including Reagan National in Virginia and Charlotte Douglas in North Carolina. Flight delays also affected LaGuardia, JFK, Newark, and Houston Bush airports. By Monday afternoon, more than 10,000 flights were either canceled or delayed as a result of the weather system. NBC News reported that areas from northern South

item.title: UC Berkeley Settles Antisemitism Lawsuit, Revises Policies on Zionist Speakers
item.description: UC Berkeley has reached a $1 million settlement to resolve a lawsuit filed by two Jewish groups over alleged antisemitic incidents following the 2023 Hamas-Israel conflict. The settlement mandates that the university prohibit student organization bylaws that ban Zionist speakers and implement mandatory antisemitism and anti-discrimination training for staff and faculty. UC Berkeley will also update its nondiscrimination web page to reference the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance (IHRA) definition of antisemitism, which includes examples such as hatred toward Jewish individuals or denying the Jewish people's right to self-determination. The university emphasized that students may still select speakers based on viewpoints but cannot adopt bylaws that explicitly exclude certain groups. The lawsuit arose after student groups at the law school restricted invitations to speakers who supported Israel, creating what plaintiffs described as a hostile environment for Jewish students.

item.title: Grif’s ‘Equinox’ Series Transforms Urban Landscapes into Digital Gardens
item.description: The Moss and Fog article “Watch a Porsche Dissolve into Flower Petals” presents digital artist Grif’s new body of work titled the “Equinox” series, where nature reclaims the human-built environment through surreal digital transformations. In this visually poetic collection, structures such as cars, brownstones, basketball courts, and even classical artworks succumb to organic overgrowth, blending steel and asphalt with blooming life forms. The opening piece, 'Going Green,' depicts a sports car abandoned in the desert gradually enveloped by roses and wildflowers, turning technology into a living garden. Other works, like 'Brooklyn Brownstone,' imagine New York’s architecture transformed into vibrant vertical jungles, exploring urban longing for nature in crowded cityscapes. 'Angels Gate' presents a basketball court taken over by flowering plants that grow along painted lines, suggesting coexistence rather than domination. In a reinterpretation of Vermeer’s 'Girl with a Pearl Earring,' G

item.title: U.S. Border Patrol Commander Gregory Bovino to Retire After Controversial Immigration Crackdowns
item.description:
Gregory Bovino, a prominent U.S. Border Patrol commander closely associated with aggressive immigration enforcement under former President Donald Trump, is reportedly retiring at the end of March. Known by critics as 'Little Napoleon,' Bovino became a highly visible figure during federal crackdowns in cities like Los Angeles and Minneapolis. His leadership style emphasized rapid, forceful operations, which he described as 'turn and burn' tactics—quickly moving from one enforcement target to another to maximize impact.
Bovino’s tenure has been marked by both praise and controversy. Supporters describe him as a bold and articulate leader willing to push boundaries, while critics argue his methods escalated tensions and contributed to public unrest. His operations often triggered protests and backlash from local officials and civil rights groups, particularly in major cities.
His role in Minneapolis drew intense scrutiny following the fatal shootings of ICU nurse Alex Pretti and Renee G

item.title: 4chan Refuses to Pay UK Online Safety Fine, Citing First Amendment Protections
item.description: The UK’s media regulator, Ofcom, has fined the online message board 4chan roughly $700,000 (about £520,000) for failing to comply with the UK’s Online Safety Act. The penalty includes $65,000 for not assessing the risk of illegal content and $25,000 for not outlining sufficient protections for users. Ofcom claims 4chan breached key provisions requiring age verification and risk management to protect minors from harmful or illegal content. However, the site openly mocked the ruling, responding with an AI-generated cartoon of a hamster posted on X (formerly Twitter) by a lawyer representing the company, who stated that 4chan operates exclusively in the United States and is protected by the First Amendment. 4chan has ignored similar fines in the past, signaling that it will not comply with UK enforcement efforts or remit payment. Ofcom’s director, Suzanne Cater, defended the decision, stating that digital platforms must safeguard minors just as physical businesses are required to with res

item.title: UK Unemployment Holds at Five-Year High Driven by Youth Job Losses
item.description:
The United Kingdom's unemployment rate has remained at its highest level in five years, standing at 5.2% in the three months leading up to January 2026, according to figures released by the Office for National Statistics (ONS). The ONS attributed much of the rise to higher unemployment among young people aged 18 to 24. Within this group, the unemployment rate reached 14.5%, while the share of young people not in full-time education and without work climbed to 19.2%, the highest level since 2014. Analysts at the Resolution Foundation noted that these figures are particularly concerning, as they suggest a continued struggle for younger workers to find stable employment opportunities.
Gender disparities also persist: male unemployment sits at 5.5%, compared to 4.8% among women. Economists link the broader rise in unemployment to increasing costs for employers, including higher national insurance contributions introduced last April, as well as a rise in minimum wages for younger workers.

item.title: Dancing Robot Causes Minor Chaos at Cupertino Hot Pot Restaurant
item.description: At a Haidilao hot pot restaurant in Cupertino, California, a humanoid robot designed for entertainment became the center of unexpected chaos when a dance performance took a wild turn. The robot—believed to be an AgiBot X2 model previously showcased at CES 2026—began flailing its arms uncontrollably, knocking dishes and chopsticks off nearby tables. Staff members rushed to restrain the machine, with at least three employees struggling to subdue it as one tried to use a mobile app to stop its movements. Although no one was seriously injured, the scene raised concerns about safety in human-robot interactions, especially given the presence of hot soup pots that could have caused burns. Haidilao later confirmed the incident but denied that the robot malfunctioned, explaining that a guest had asked for a closer view, forcing it to perform in a tighter space than usual, which interfered with its movement sensors. The company emphasized that this was a matter of operational setting, not a syst

item.title: Gemini reports wider fourth‑quarter loss as crypto price slump weighs on revenue and trading volumes
item.description: Gemini, the cryptocurrency exchange founded by the Winklevoss twins, reported a wider net loss in the fourth quarter as steep declines in the prices of major digital assets weighed heavily on its results. The company’s revenue fell notably from the previous quarter, reflecting weaker trading volumes and a broader pullback in investor activity across the crypto market. The fourth‑quarter loss was driven by a combination of lower fees from spot and derivatives trading, higher operating and infrastructure costs, and write‑downs linked to holdings in certain crypto assets that lost significant value during the period. Market participants also noted that the broader digital‑asset ecosystem saw a sharp pullback in liquidity and investor appetite, exacerbating volatility and compressing profit margins for exchanges. Management said it is focusing on cost discipline, tightening its balance sheet, and exploring new product lines, including regulated financial products, to diversify revenue stre

item.title: UK Introduces Major Steel Tariffs and Industry Support Plan
item.description: The United Kingdom has unveiled a new Steel Strategy that marks a major shift toward protectionism in its trade and industrial policy. The plan allocates around £2.5 billion to support domestic steel producers, including both nationalized plants like British Steel in Scunthorpe and private manufacturers across the country aiming to produce lower-carbon steel. One of its key objectives is to increase the proportion of domestically made steel used in the UK from a record low of 30% to 50%, aiming to revive the national industry’s role in infrastructure and renewable energy projects such as wind turbines. However, the most consequential change is the introduction of a new 50% tariff on certain imported steel products—particularly cheap galvanized steel from countries like Vietnam and Turkey, whose producers benefit from subsidies unavailable to British companies. These tariffs replace older, weaker trade measures set to expire soon and substantially raise Britain’s trade barriers for the

item.title: Men's Growing Pursuit of Elevated Testosterone Levels
item.description: A growing number of men, influenced by the manosphere and figures like Joe Rogan and Robert F. Kennedy Jr., are fixated on achieving 'High T'—elevated testosterone levels. Mark Holman, once skinny and depressed with 622 ng/dL, naturally boosted his to 1,104 ng/dL through diet (eggs, red meat, oysters), supplements (tongkat ali, fenugreek), and weight training, reporting enhanced physique, confidence, and libido. Testosterone replacement therapy (TRT) prescriptions surged to 11 million in the US by 2024, up from 7.3 million in 2019. Men now routinely test and compare levels, akin to fitness stats, amid declining average T levels linked to obesity and inactivity. While proponents cite benefits like reduced depression and vitality, experts caution against pathologizing healthy young men. TRT risks include infertility, testicular shrinkage, and dependency; natural peaks max at 1,400 ng/dL, versus steroid excesses over 3,000 ng/dL causing 'roid rage.' Political ties emerge, with Trump suppo

item.title: FCC Official Involved in Potential Overreach Targeting Disney
item.description: Records have revealed that an official within the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) allegedly offered assistance to Brendan Carr, a Republican commissioner, in targeting Disney. The documents indicate that the FCC enforcement chief explored options for potentially penalizing Disney over content deemed objectionable. This action has sparked concerns about potential political interference and overreach within the regulatory agency. Brendan Carr, known for his outspoken criticisms of Disney's programming, reportedly sought ways to leverage the FCC's authority to exert pressure on the media giant. The incident highlights the delicate balance between regulatory oversight and potential political motivations.
